description: Known content-based discovery failures to track and improve over time. Listed failures are reported in the benchmark but do not block the exit code.

failures.reason: After HTML reduction, the 'Aisle 7, Shelf B' store location text loses context. The LLM doesn't associate it with an in-store location action.
On a product detail page, the LLM sees addToCart as sufficient and excludes buyProduct as redundant. Both are valid — buyProduct handles the broader 'buy this' intent.
After HTML reduction, the search input loses identifying attributes. The LLM doesn't recognize it as a product search capability.

designIssues.description: LLM excludes multi-step flows that can navigate to the right context
designIssues.example: buyProduct excluded on homedepot.com homepage because no product is visible, but the flow handles this by searching first
designIssues.rootCause: The LLM prompt asks which actions 'can actually be performed on THIS page' — it doesn't know that flows can navigate away from the current page
designIssues.proposedFix: Update the LLM prompt to distinguish between direct-action flows (need matching page elements) and navigating flows (can search/navigate to the right context from any page on the site). Include flow descriptions in the schema so the LLM can see that buyProduct 'searches for a product and adds it to cart'.
